+## LESSONS LEARNED (ALL CRITICAL)
+1. S1 stopped converting at $6 due to raised quality expectations
+2. Competitor events (~0.01 each) permanently raise expectations, accumulating
+3. Quality DECAYS without dev spending (global bonus drops ~0.004/week)
+4. S2/S3 subscribers are MASSIVE money losers (compute cost >> revenue)
+5. Only S1 at >$14/mo is profitable (low usage = profitable)
+6. Existing sub prices are FROZEN at signup - set_prices doesn't change them
+7. Lowering model tier would trigger mass churn of existing profitable subs
+8. Tenure loyalty keeps existing subs somewhat tolerant (~+0.05 quality)
+9. R&D is the only path to meaningful quality boost but costs $500K+
+10. In-app ads degrades quality → more churn → not viable
+11. Lead acquisition costs $0.50/lead whether they convert or not
+12. At A=$14, S1 leads won't even try → saves acquisition costs
 
-## LESSONS LEARNED (CRITICAL)
-1. Lead promo DOES NOT affect evaluation price - only first billing
-2. Ongoing promo (set_promotion) affects billing/satisfaction, NOT lead evaluation
-3. NEW LEADS EVALUATE AT LIST PRICE ONLY (set_prices)
-4. Listed prices are FROZEN at subscription time - set_prices doesn't change existing
-5. S1 stopped converting because quality gap was only -0.0008 at $8
-6. Competitor events permanently raise quality expectations
-7. Tenure loyalty keeps existing subs (up to +0.13 quality bonus)
-8. Low-usage groups (S1) are profitable; high-usage (S3) are money losers
-9. S3-A single sub loses ~$6.37/day in compute = $191/mo
-10. Don't target high-usage groups with ads or low-price plans
-11. R&D is the only path to reaching S2/E1-E3 but costs $500K+

+- Long-term: need R&D breakthrough ($500K+) or find way to grow revenue
